Defines custom exception classes for error handling in the pulse tool of pebble-firmware.

# Purpose
This code defines a set of custom exception classes for error handling in a Python application. Each exception class inherits from a base class named `PulseError`, which itself is a subclass of the built-in `Exception` class. The specific exceptions, such as `ProtocolAlreadyRegistered`, `FrameDecodeError`, and `CommandTimedOut`, represent distinct error conditions that can occur within the application. This structure allows for precise identification and handling of different error scenarios, facilitating debugging and improving code maintainability. The code provides narrow functionality focused on error management within a specific domain or application context.
